html:
[1824]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 良久 - Means 'for a long time' or 'after a while,' indicating a passage of time.
  • 台盘到 - Likely refers to the arrival of food or a meal, possibly in a formal setting.
  • 与县令耳语 - Means 'whispered to the magistrate,' suggesting a private conversation.
  • 总煮来 - Means 'to cook all of them,' indicating that all the dishes should be prepared.
  • 某有一女子年七八岁 - Means 'I have a daughter who is seven or eight years old,' introducing a personal anecdote.
  • 常言何不令我勾当家事 - Means 'she often says why not let me manage household affairs,' indicating the daughter's desire for responsibility.
  • 昨恼渠遣检校作岁饭食 - Means 'I was angry yesterday and sent her to check the New Year's Eve food,' suggesting the magistrate's reaction to the daughter's request.
  • 有五般馄饨问煮那盘 - Means 'there are five kinds of wontons, which one should be cooked,' indicating a choice among different dishes.
  • 逡巡以大椀盛二客食尽 - Means 'soon, a large bowl was served, and the two guests ate it all,' describing the rapid consumption of the food.
  • 佐吏从外走云敕使到 - Means 'an assistant official came running from outside saying that a royal envoy had arrived,' indicating an urgent situation.
  • 旧例合迎县令惊忙揖二客鞭马而去 - Means 'according to custom, the magistrate should welcome the envoy, so he hurriedly bowed to the two guests and rode away,' describing the magistrate's quick response.
  • 遂出欲就店终飡 - Means 'they then left to find a place to finish their meal,' indicating the guests' decision to continue eating elsewhere.
  • 其仆者已归结束先发已行数里 - Means 'their servant had already returned, packed up, and left ahead, having traveled several miles,' suggesting the servant's quick departure.
  • 饮啄之分也 - Means 'it is the division of food and drink,' indicating the natural order of things.
  • 出逸史 - Means 'from the *Yishi* (逸史),' indicating the source of the story.
  • 何日肩三署终年尾百寮 - Means 'when will I serve in the three departments and not be at the end of the hundred officials all year,' expressing a desire for advancement.
  • 其后出入清要 - Means 'later, he served in important positions,' indicating a positive turn in his career.
  • 张宿遭遇除谏议大夫 - Means 'Zhang Su, upon encountering a favorable opportunity, was appointed as a censor,' providing another example.
  • 宣慰山东宪宗面许回日与相 - Means 'the emperor of the Tang dynasty personally promised that when he returned, he would make him a chancellor,' indicating a high-level appointment.
  • 至东洛都亭驿暴卒 - Means 'he died suddenly at the East Luoyang Inn,' indicating a tragic end.
  • 崔元章在举场无成 - Means 'Cui Yuanzhang, in the examination hall, achieved nothing,' indicating failure.
  • 为执权者所叹 - Means 'he was lamented by those in power,' indicating recognition of his potential.
  • 主司要约必与及第 - Means 'the examiners promised that he would surely pass,' indicating a promise of success.
  • 入试日中风不得一名 - Means 'on the day of the exam, he fell ill and failed to pass,' indicating a disappointing outcome.
  • 朴因话家世曾经之事 - Means 'Pu then recounted family history and past events,' indicating a narrative shift.
  • 朴父清故平阳太守 - Means 'Pu's father, Qing, was the former governor of Pingyang,' providing family background.
  • 建中初任蓝田尉 - Means 'in the early years of Jianzhong, he served as an official in Lantian,' providing a specific historical context.
  • 德宗初即位用法严峻 - Means 'Emperor Dezong, upon ascending the throne, enforced strict laws,' indicating a political context.
  • 是月三日之内大臣出贬者七 - Means 'within three days of that month, seven ministers were demoted,' indicating a period of political purges.
  • 中途赐死者三 - Means 'three were executed along the way,' indicating the severity of the punishments.
  • 刘晏黎干皆是其数 - Means 'Liu Yan and Li Gan were among them,' providing specific names of those affected.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 县令 - Refers to the local magistrate, a key figure in the administrative hierarchy.
  • 台盘 - Likely refers to a formal dining table or setting.
  • 佐吏 - Refers to assistant officials, who assist the magistrate in his duties.
  • 敕使 - Refers to a royal envoy, indicating a high-level official sent by the emperor.
  • 旧例 - Refers to established customs or traditions.
  • 三署 - Refers to three important government departments.
  • 百寮 - Refers to the hundred officials, indicating a large number of subordinates.
  • 谏议大夫 - Refers to a censor, a position responsible for advising the emperor and criticizing government policies.
  • 宣慰 - Refers to the act of consoling or comforting, often used in the context of official duties.
  • 宪宗 - Refers to Emperor Dezong of the Tang dynasty.
  • 东洛都亭驿 - Refers to the East Luoyang Inn, a specific location in the story.
  • 举场 - Refers to the examination hall, where candidates take the imperial exams.
  • 执权者 - Refers to those in power, such as high-ranking officials.
  • 主司 - Refers to the examiners, who oversee the imperial exams.
  • 中风 - Refers to a sudden illness, often used to indicate a failure to perform well.
  • 建中 - Refers to the reign title of Emperor Dezong of the Tang dynasty.
  • 蓝田尉 - Refers to an official position in Lantian, a county in Shaanxi province.
  • 德宗 - Refers to Emperor Dezong of the Tang dynasty.
  • 刘晏 - Refers to a historical figure, a prominent official during the Tang dynasty.
  • 黎干 - Refers to a historical figure, another prominent official during the Tang dynasty.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 良久台盘到有一小奴与县令耳语 - This sentence describes a sequence of events: after a long time, a small servant whispered to the magistrate.
  • 县令曰总煮来 - This is a direct speech from the magistrate, instructing the servant to cook all the dishes.
  • 谓二客曰某有一女子年七八岁常言何不令我勾当家事 - This is a direct speech from the magistrate to the two guests, introducing a personal anecdote.
  • 昨恼渠遣检校作岁饭食适来云有五般馄饨问煮那盘 - This sentence describes the magistrate's actions and the daughter's request.
  • 逡巡以大椀盛二客食尽 - This sentence describes the rapid consumption of the food by the two guests.
  • 佐吏从外走云敕使到旧例合迎县令惊忙揖二客鞭马而去 - This sentence describes the urgent arrival of the royal envoy and the magistrate's quick response.
  • 遂出欲就店终飡其仆者已归结束先发已行数里 - This sentence describes the guests' decision to continue eating elsewhere and the servant's quick departure.

[1825]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 贬道州司戸叅军 - Formal title meaning 'reduced to the position of Chief Clerk of the Household Bureau in Daozhou.'
  • 自朝受责驰驿出城不得归第 - Means 'from the morning, after receiving blame, he was dispatched by relay to leave the city and was unable to return home.'
  • 虑耗达妻闻惊必至不起 - Means 'Yan feared that if the news reached his wife, she would be shocked and might not recover.'
  • 炎夕次蓝田 - Means 'Yan stopped at Lantian in the evening.'
  • 屈崔少府相见 - Means 'Yan bent to meet Cui, the Magistrate.'
  • 欲奉烦为申辞疾请假一日发一急脚附书寛两处相忧以候其来耗便当首路可乎 - Means 'I wish to trouble you to submit a request for a day of leave due to illness on my behalf. To send a fast runner with a letter to alleviate the worries of both places while waiting for news of her condition, so I can then set out on the road, is that acceptable?'
  • 清许之 - Means 'Qing agreed.'
  • 邮知事吕华进而言曰 - Means 'Lü Hua, the postal official, stepped forward and said.'
  • 此故不可敕命严迅 - Means 'This is certainly not possible. The imperial command is urgent.'
  • 清谓吕华杨侍郎迫切不然 - Means 'Qing told Lü Hua that Yang, the Attendant, was not so urgent.'
  • 申府以阙马可乎 - Means 'To request the prefecture for a horse, is that acceptable?'
  • 华久而对曰 - Means 'Lü Hua replied after a long pause.'
  • 此即可矣 - Means 'This is possible.'
  • 清于是以此闻于京府 - Means 'Qing then reported this to the capital prefecture.'
  • 又自出俸钱二十千买细氊令造氊舁顾夫直诣炎宅取炎夫人 - Means 'He also spent twenty thousand coins of his salary to buy fine felt. To have a felt litter made. To send a servant to Yan's residence to fetch his wife.'
  • 夫人扶病登舁仍戒其丁勤夜行旦日达蓝田时炎 - Means 'The wife, supporting herself with illness, climbed onto the litter. She still instructed the servant to travel diligently at night. By dawn, they reached Lantian, where Yan was.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道州 - Refers to Daozhou, a prefecture in southern China.
  • 司戸叅军 - Refers to the Chief Clerk of the Household Bureau, a low-ranking official.
  • 蓝田 - Refers to Lantian, a county in Shaanxi Province.
  • 崔少府 - Refers to Cui, the Magistrate, a local official.
  • 杨侍郎 - Refers to Yang, the Attendant, a high-ranking official.
  • 京府 - Refers to the capital prefecture.
  • 氊舁 - Refers to a felt litter, a mode of transport for the sick or elderly.
  • 丁勤 - Refers to diligent servants.

Proper Nouns

  • 道州 - The name of a prefecture.
  • 司戸叅军 - The title of a low-ranking official.
  • 蓝田 - The name of a county.
  • 崔少府 - The title of a local official.
  • 杨侍郎 - The title of a high-ranking official.
  • 京府 - The name of the capital prefecture.
  • 氊舁 - The name of a felt litter.
  • 丁勤 - The name of diligent servants.

[1826]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 行李简约 - Indicates that the traveler's belongings were minimal, possibly due to illness or other constraints.
  • 便与炎偕往 - Means 'and thus went together with Yan,' indicating a decision to travel together.
  • 执清之手问第行 - A gesture of holding someone's hand to ask about their well-being or intentions, here specifically about the order of the journey.
  • 某第十八 - A response indicating that the person being addressed is the eighteenth in a sequence or list.
  • 率俸钱数千 - Means 'took several thousand in salary,' indicating the use of personal funds for travel expenses.
  • 具商于己来山程之费 - Means 'provided for the expenses of the journey to the mountain,' showing the preparation of necessary funds.
  • 至韩公驿执清之袂 - Indicates arriving at the Han Gong post station and taking hold of Qing's sleeve, a gesture of farewell or greeting.
  • 令妻出见曰 - Means 'had his wife come out to meet and say,' showing a formal introduction.
  • 此崔十八死生不相忘无复多言矣 - A statement of deep friendship and mutual commitment, indicating that despite life and death, they will not forget each other and there is no need for further words.
  • 炎至商于洛源驿 - Means 'Yan arrived at the Shangyu Luya post station,' indicating the next stop in the journey.
  • 马乏驿仆王新送骡一头 - Means 'the horse was tired, and the post station servant Wang Xin provided a mule,' showing assistance during the journey.
  • 又逢道州司仓叅军李全方挽运入奏 - Means 'and encountered Daozhou Sikuang Official Li Quanfang, who was transporting goods to the imperial court,' indicating a chance meeting.
  • 全方辄倾囊以济炎 - Means 'Quanfang immediately emptied his bag to help Yan,' showing generosity.
  • 行李后二年秋炎自江华除中书侍郎入相 - Means 'two years later in autumn, Yan was appointed as the Minister of the Secretariat and entered the government,' indicating a significant career advancement.
  • 还至京兆界问驿使崔十八郎在否 - Means 'on returning to the territory of Jingzhao, he asked the post station official if Cui the Eighteenth was there,' showing a return and inquiry.
  • 驿吏答曰在炎喜甚顷之清迎谒于前 - Means 'the post station official replied that he was there, and Yan was very happy; soon after, Qing came forward to greet him,' indicating a reunion.
  • 炎便止之曰崔十八郎不合如此相待今日生还乃是子之恩也 - Means 'Yan stopped him and said, 'Cui the Eighteenth should not have treated me like this; my return today is your kindness,' showing gratitude and acknowledgment of the past.
  • 仍连镳而行话湘楚气候因曰足下之才何适不可老夫今日可以力 - Means 'they continued to ride together, talking about the climate of Xiang and Chu, and Yan said, 'Your talent is suited for anything; today I can help,' indicating a conversation about the environment and the capabilities of the other person.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 行李简约妻亦病 - Indicates the state of travel and health, reflecting the practical aspects of travel in ancient times.
  • 炎至商于洛源驿 - Refers to specific locations along the journey, which are historically significant.
  • 马乏驿仆王新送骡一头 - Shows the support system in place for travelers, including post stations and their staff.
  • 又逢道州司仓叅军李全方挽运入奏 - Indicates the presence of officials and the logistics of the imperial court.
  • 全方辄倾囊以济炎 - Reflects the cultural value of generosity and mutual aid.
  • 炎自江华除中书侍郎入相 - Marks a significant political event, showing the rise of Yan in the government.
  • 还至京兆界问驿使崔十八郎在否 - Indicates the return to a central location and the importance of the post station system.
  • 炎便止之曰崔十八郎不合如此相待今日生还乃是子之恩也 - Shows the importance of personal relationships and the value of gratitude in Chinese culture.

Proper Nouns

  • 炎 - Refers to Yan, a person in the narrative.
  • 清 - Refers to Qing, another person in the narrative.
  • 崔十八 - Refers to Cui the Eighteenth, a person in the narrative.

[1827]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 致栢台谏署唯所择 - Yang Yan appointed [someone] to the Office of the Imperial Censors at his discretion.
  • 无敢希侥幸意 - [Someone] declined, not daring to hope for any favor.
  • 炎又曰勿疑但言之 - Yang Yan said, 'Do not doubt, just say it.'
  • 炎居相位十日追洛源驿 - Yang Yan, in his position as chancellor, followed [someone] to the Luoyuan Post Station for ten days.
  • 王新为中书主事仍奏授鄂州唐年县尉 - Wang Xin was appointed to the position of Zongshu Zhushi and was also appointed by imperial decree to the position of County Magistrate of Tangnian in Ezhou.
  • 李全方监察御史仍知商州洛源监 - Li Quanfang was appointed as a Censor and was also given the responsibility of overseeing the Luoyuan Supervision in Shangzhou.
  • 清之所约沉然 - Qing, who had previously made an agreement, remained silent.
  • 清罢职特就炎第谒之 - Qing, after being dismissed from office, specially went to Yang Yan's residence to pay a visit.
  • 炎则已有怠色 - Yang Yan, however, already showed a look of weariness.
  • 清从此退居不复措意 - Qing, from then on, withdrew and no longer took any action.
  • 后二年再贬崖州 - Two years later, [Qing] was demoted again to Yizhou.
  • 至蓝田喟然太息若者 - When [Qing] reached Lantian, he sighed deeply as if in despair.
  • 使人召清清辞疾不往 - Yang Yan sent someone to summon Qing, but Qing declined on the grounds of illness and did not go.
  • 乃自咎曰杨炎可以死矣 - Qing then blamed himself, saying, 'Yang Yan should die.'
  • 竟不还他崔清官 - Finally, [Qing] did not return to him, the official position of Cui Qing.
  • 出续定命录 - This story is recorded in the *Xu Dingming Lu* (Continued Records of Destiny).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 栢台谏署 - Refers to the Office of the Imperial Censors, an important institution in the Tang Dynasty responsible for advising the emperor and monitoring officials.
  • 杨炎 - A prominent statesman and chancellor during the Tang Dynasty, known for his reforms and political influence.
  • 崔清 - Another historical figure, likely an official or scholar, whose interactions with Yang Yan are described in the text.
  • 中书主事 - A position in the central government, responsible for drafting and implementing imperial edicts.
  • 监察御史 - A title for a censor, responsible for monitoring the conduct of officials and reporting misconduct to the emperor.
  • 崖州 - A place name, likely referring to a remote or distant location, often used in historical texts to indicate exile or demotion.
  • 蓝田 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location in China, possibly associated with the events described in the text.
  • 续定命录 - The title of the source text, which may be a collection of anecdotes or records about fates and destinies, possibly compiled by a later author.

Proper Nouns

  • 栢台 - The Office of the Imperial Censors.
  • 谏署 - The office of censors.
  • 杨炎 - A prominent statesman and chancellor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 崔清 - Another historical figure, likely an official or scholar.
  • 中书主事 - A position in the central government.
  • 监察御史 - A title for a censor.
  • 崖州 - A place name, likely referring to a remote or distant location.
  • 蓝田 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location in China.
  • 续定命录 - The title of the source text, which may be a collection of anecdotes or records about fates and destinies.

[1828]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 未有宦名 - Had not yet achieved official success.
  • 寄托崔氏 - Entrusted to the Cui family.
  • 纱笼中人 - A person in the silk screen, a metaphor for someone of high status.
  • 畏迍厄 - To fear misfortune.
  • 请问 - To ask questions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李相藩 - Li Fan, a prominent official during the Tang Dynasty, known for his administrative skills and integrity.
  • 崔庶子 - Cui Shuzi, a high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty, and his family was influential.
  • 胡芦生 - Hu Lusheng, a well-known fortune-teller in the Tang Dynasty, famous for his unique method of divination.
  • 脑疮 - A medical condition in the Tang Dynasty, often associated with serious illness and potential death.
  • 防钱 - A form of offering or contribution, often used in religious or superstitious contexts.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • Break down the long, complex sentence into smaller, more readable segments while maintaining the logical flow and coherence of the narrative.

Proper Nouns

  • 李相藩 - Li Fan, a high-ranking official.
  • 崔庶子 - Cui Shuzi, a high-ranking official.
  • 胡芦生 - Hu Lusheng, a fortune-teller.
  • 脑疮 - Brain abscess.
  • 防钱 - Offering or contribution.

[1829]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 纱笼之事 - Refers to a specific event or matter related to '纱笼' (possibly a type of garment or metaphorical term); likely a key plot point.
  • 昼寝梦有一人召出城外 - Dreamed of being called out of the city; suggests a supernatural or symbolic event.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 纱笼 - Could refer to a type of garment or a metaphorical term; exact meaning requires further research.
  • 参佐桥 - A specific location in Yangzhou, likely a bridge or a place where officials or guests would stay.
  • 员外 - A title for a high-ranking official or a wealthy person.
  • 藩 - Refers to the governor or a high-ranking official.
  • 李三郎 - A person named Ruan Sanlang, likely a minor official or someone of lower rank.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '纱笼之事终不说遂往扬州居于参佐桥使院中' is elliptical and requires interpretation: the matter related to '纱笼' was never resolved, and the person then went to Yangzhou and settled in the guesthouse at Zhenzuo Bridge.
  • The sentence '使院中有一高员外与藩往还甚熟' is straightforward but requires understanding of the context of the guesthouse and the relationship between the high-ranking official and the governor.
  • The sentence '一旦来诣藩既去际晚又至李公甚讶之' is elliptical and requires interpretation: the person came to visit the governor once, left, and returned later, surprising Mr. Li.
  • The sentence '既相见高曰朝来拜候却归困甚昼寝梦有一人召出城外于荆中行见旧使庄戸卒已十年' is complex and requires breaking down the sequence of events: the person met Mr. Gao, who said he came to pay respects in the morning but returned feeling very tired, took a nap, and dreamed of someone calling him out of the city, and while walking in the thicket, he saw the old servant's house, which had been there for ten years.
  • The sentence '谓某曰员外不合至此为物所诱且便须回某送员外去却引至城门某谓之曰汝安得在此云我为小吏差与李三郎当直' is a dialogue and requires careful interpretation of the sequence of events and the meaning of the terms used.
  • The sentence '某曰何处李三郎曰住参佐桥知员外与李三郎往还故此只候某曰三郎安得如此曰是纱笼中人诘之不肯言因曰某饥员外能与少酒饭钱财否乎城不敢入请与城外置之某谓曰就三郎宅' is a continuation of the dialogue and requires careful interpretation of the sequence of events and the meaning of the terms used.

Proper Nouns

  • 纱笼 - A specific term or concept that requires further research.
  • 参佐桥 - A specific location in Yangzhou.
  • 员外 - A title for a high-ranking official or a wealthy person.
  • 藩 - Refers to the governor or a high-ranking official.
  • 李三郎 - A person named Ruan Sanlang, likely a minor official or someone of lower rank.

[1830]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 中得否 - Formal question meaning 'Is it correct?' or 'Is it the case?'
  • 若如此是杀某也 - Hypothetical statement meaning 'If it is like this, it would be like killing me.'
  • 遂觉已令于城外与置酒食 - Means 'So I immediately ordered to prepare food and drink outside the city.'
  • 且奉报好消息 - Means 'And I will report the good news.'
  • 李公微笑数年 - Means 'Li Gong smiled for several years.'
  • 奏李公为巡官校书郎 - Means 'He nominated Li Gong as a circuit inspector and a proofreader.'
  • 会有新罗僧能相人 - Means 'There was a Silla monk who could read people's fortunes.'
  • 且言张公不得为宰相 - Means 'And he said that Zhang Gong would not become a chancellor.'
  • 甚怀怏 - Means 'He was very displeased.'
  • 因令于使院中看郎官有得为宰相者否 - Means 'So he ordered to look in the office to see if any of the officials could become chancellors.'
  • 遍视良久 - Means 'He looked around for a long time.'
  • 并无 - Means 'There were none.'
  • 莫有郎官未入院否 - Means 'Is there any official who has not entered the office yet?'
  • 报云李巡官未入 - Means 'They reported that Li Gong had not entered yet.'
  • 便令促召 - Means 'So he ordered to call him urgently.'
  • 逡巡至 - Means 'He arrived shortly.'
  • 僧降阶迎 - Means 'The monk descended the steps to greet him.'
  • 谓张公曰巡官是纱笼中人 - Means 'He said to Zhang Gong, 'The official is a person in the silk cage.'
  • 仆射且不及 - Means 'Even the Vice-Chancellor is not enough.'
  • 大喜 - Means 'He was very happy.'
  • 因问纱笼中之事 - Means 'So he asked about the silk cage.'
  • 僧曰宰相防司必潜纱笼防之 - Means 'The monk said that the chancellor's office must be hidden in the silk cage to protect it.'
  • 恐为异物所扰 - Means 'To prevent it from being disturbed by strange things.'
  • 余官即不得也 - Means 'Other officials cannot do this.'
  • 方悟胡芦生及高所说 - Means 'Only then did he realize what Hu Lu Sheng and Gao had said.'
  • 李公竟为宰相也 - Means 'Li Gong eventually became a chancellor.'
  • 信哉人之贵贱分定矣 - Means 'Indeed, a person's status is predetermined.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 新罗僧 - Refers to a monk from Silla, an ancient kingdom in the Korean Peninsula.
  • 相人 - The practice of fortune-telling based on physical appearance.
  • 张建封 - A historical figure, the Governor of Yangzhou during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 巡官校书郎 - Titles of officials in the Tang Dynasty, referring to a circuit inspector and a proofreader.
  • 纱笼中人 - A metaphor for a person of high status or importance, often used to describe a chancellor.
  • 胡芦生及高 - Refers to historical figures or stories that provide context for the narrative.

Proper Nouns

  • 李公 - Refers to Li Gong, the main character.
  • 张建封 - Refers to Zhang Jianfeng, the Governor of Yangzhou.
  • 新罗僧 - Refers to a monk from Silla.
  • 胡芦生及高 - Refers to historical figures or stories.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '有進士李岳連舉不第夜夢人謂曰頭上有山何以得上第及覺不可名獄遂更名言果中第' can be broken down into: 'There was a scholar named Li Yue who failed the imperial examinations repeatedly. One night, he dreamed that someone told him, 'With a mountain on your head, how can you achieve the top rank?' Upon waking, he couldn't name the reason, so he changed his name to 'Yan.' He finally succeeded in passing the examination.'
  • The sentence '王沐者涯之再從弟也家於江南老且窮以涯執相權遂跨蹇驢而至京師索米僦舍住三十日始得一見涯於門屏所望不過一簿一尉耳而涯見沐潦倒無雁序情太和九年秋沐方説涯之嬖奴以導所欲擬許以微官處焉自是旦夕造涯之門以俟其命及涯就誅仇士良收捕家人時沐方在涯私第謂其王氏之黨遂不免於腰領' can be broken down into: 'Wang Mu, the second cousin of Wang Ya, lived in southern Jiangnan, old and poor. Because Wang Ya held the power of the chancellor, he rode a slow donkey to the capital city, sought rice, and rented a room, living there for thirty days before finally seeing Wang Ya at the gate. He expected nothing more than a minor clerk or a low-ranking official, but Wang Ya saw Wang Mu's destitution and lack of family support. In the autumn of Taiping Nine Year, Wang Mu flattered Wang Ya's favorite eunuch to achieve his desires. He planned to grant him a minor official position. From then on, he visited Wang Ya's door every day, waiting for his command. When Wang Ya was executed and Chou Shiliang arrested his family members, at that time, Wang Mu was still in Wang Ya's private residence. He was considered a member of the Wang clan and thus could not escape punishment.'
  • The sentence '舒守謙元舆之族聰敏慧悟富有春秋元舆禮遇颇至十年元舆處之猶子薦取明經第官厯校書郎及持相印許為曹郎命之忽以非過怒謙至朔旦伏謁頓不能見由是日加谴责為僮僕輕易謙既不自安遂置書於門下辭往江表而元舆亦不問翌日辦裝出長安咨嗟蹇分惆悵自失即駐馬望涕泗涟如及昭應聞元舆之禍方始釋然當時論者以王舒禍福之異定分焉' can be broken down into: 'Shu Shouqian, a member of the Yu family, was intelligent, wise, and had many years ahead of him. Yu treated him with great respect. For ten years, Yu treated him like a son. He recommended him to take the Mingjing examination and serve as a clerk in the Imperial Library. When Yu held the seal of the chancellor, he promised to appoint him as a secretary. Suddenly, without any fault, he became angry with Shu Shouqian. On the first day of the month, when Shu Shouqian knelt to pay respects, he could not see him. From then on, he was criticized daily and treated like a servant. Shu Shouqian, feeling uncomfortable, left a letter at the door and decided to go to the south. But Yu did not ask about it. The next day, he prepared his luggage and left Chang'an. He sighed about his bad luck and felt disheartened. He stopped his horse and looked back, tears streaming down his face. When he heard about Yu's disaster, he finally felt relieved. At that time, people discussed the different fortunes of Wang and Shu, determining their fate.'

  • 乃夢中相隨向東京履信坊十字街西道北有一家入宅内東行屋下正見一婦人生一女子 - Describes a dream where the protagonist follows a woman to a specific location in the Eastern Capital (Dongjing, modern-day Kaifeng), where he sees a woman giving birth to a daughter.
  • 此是君婦 - Means 'this is your wife.'
  • 崔公驚寤殊不信之 - Means 'Cui Gong woke up in shock and did not believe it.'

  • 自此后官至四品年五十八乃婚侍郎韦陟堂妹年始十九虽嫌崔公之年竟嫁之 - Indicates that after this, Cui Gong reached the rank of fourth-grade official at age fifty-eight and married the nineteen-year-old daughter of a Langzhong (a high-ranking official) named Wei Zhi, despite the age difference.
  • 乃于履信坊韦家宅上成亲果在东行屋下居住寻勘歳月正是所梦之日其妻适生崔公至三品年九十韦夫人与之偕老向四十年食其贵禄也 - Indicates that the marriage took place at Wei Zhi's residence in Lüxìn Lane, and the location matched the dream. Cui Gong lived to be ninety years old, reaching the rank of third-grade official, and his wife lived with him for forty years, enjoying the benefits of his high rank.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long, complex sentence '忽假寐見人云此家女非君之婦君婦今日始生乃夢中相隨向東京履信坊十字街西道北有一家入宅内東行屋下正見一婦人生一女子云此是君婦' must be read as a single dream narrative.
  • The long, complex sentence '崔公驚寤殊不信之俄而所平章女忽然暴亡自此后官至四品年五十八乃婚侍郎韦陟堂妹年始十九虽嫌崔公之年竟嫁之' must be read as a sequence of events.
  • The long, complex sentence '乃于履信坊韦家宅上成亲果在东行屋下居住寻勘歳月正是所梦之日其妻适生崔公至三品年九十韦夫人与之偕老向四十年食其贵禄也' must be read as a sequence of events and a reflection on the dream.

  • 十字街西道北 - A specific location in the lane.

  • 君之妻也 - A formal way of referring to someone as 'your wife' or 'the wife of the lord.'

  • 后二年始生 - 'Two years later, she was born.' This phrase describes the birth of a child after a delay.
  • 生十七年而君娶 - 'Seventeen years after she was born, you married.' This phrase indicates a significant time gap between the birth of the child and the marriage.
  • 时当官未逾年而韦氏卒 - 'At the time, you had not yet completed a year in office, and Wei died.' This phrase provides context about the timing of Wei's death.
  • 殷异其言 - 'Yin was surprised by what he heard.' This phrase indicates a reaction to unexpected news.
  • 因问郑氏之夫曰 - 'He then asked the husband of Zheng's family.' This phrase describes an action taken to seek more information.
  • 即同郡郭子元也 - 'He is the same as the fellow townsman Guo Ziyuan.' This phrase identifies a person.
  • 子元娶五年而卒 - 'Ziyuan married for five years and then died.' This phrase provides details about Ziyuan's marriage and death.
  • 然将嫁之夕君其梦之 - 'However, on the night before the wedding, you dreamed of her.' This phrase describes a dream that occurred before the wedding.
  • 既三年殷下第 - 'After three years, Yin failed the examination.' This phrase provides context about Yin's failure in an examination.
  • 有内黄人郭绍家富于财 - 'There was a person from Neihuang named Guo Shao who was wealthy.' This phrase introduces a new character.
  • 闻郑氏美纳赂以求其婚 - 'He heard that Zheng was beautiful and offered bribes to seek her hand in marriage.' This phrase describes the actions taken by Guo Shao.
  • 郑氏之母聚族谋曰 - 'Zheng's mother gathered the family and said.' This phrase describes a family discussion.
  • 女年既笄殷未成事吾老矣且愿见有所适今有郭绍者求娶吾欲许之何如 - 'Her daughter is of marriageable age, and Yin has not yet married. I am old and wish to see her settled. Now there is Guo Shao who seeks to marry her. Should I agree?' This phrase provides the mother's reasoning and question.
  • 诸子曰唯命 - 'The sons said, 'As you command.' This phrase shows the sons' agreement with the mother's decision.
  • 郑氏闻之泣恚将断发为尼者数四 - 'Zheng heard this and cried in anger, several times threatening to cut her hair and become a nun.' This phrase describes Zheng's reaction.
  • 及嫁之夕忽得疾昏若将不救 - 'On the night of the wedding, she suddenly fell ill and seemed to be near death.' This phrase describes a sudden illness.

  • 其后皆验如勾龙生之言尔 - 'Afterwards, everything happened as Goulong had predicted.' This phrase concludes the story with a sense of fulfillment.

  • 吾以丙申之年当有石氏王此地者 - A prophecy based on astrological calculations, predicting a future ruler.
  • 移四字中两竖画置天字左右即丙字也 - A method of interpreting the inscription, suggesting a hidden message.
  • 移四之外围以十字贯之即申字也 - Another method of interpretation, reinforcing the prophecy.
  • 晋高祖以石姓起并州 - A historical reference to the founding emperor of the Jin dynasty, who had the surname Shi and rose to power in Bingzhou.
  • 如鸿之言 - Confirms the accuracy of the prophecy, validating Xu Hong's interpretation.
  • 出稽神录 - Indicates the source of the story, the *Ji Shen Lu* (Records of Divine Phenomena), a collection of supernatural stories.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 竹 - A compound word referring to a specific type of bamboo-eating rodent; '' is a character not commonly used in modern Chinese, possibly a variant or archaic form.
  • 巨如野貍 - Literally 'as large as a wild fox,' used to describe the size of the bamboo rat.
  • 其肉肥脆 - Describes the meat of the bamboo rat as both fatty and tender.
  • 山民重之 - '山民' refers to people living in the mountains, and '重之' means they value or prize the bamboo rat.
  • 每发地取之甚艰 - '发地' means to dig up the ground, and '甚艰' means very difficult, indicating that catching the bamboo rat is a challenging task.
  • 岐梁睚眦之年 - '岐梁' refers to the regions of Qilang (Qin and Liang), and '睚眦之年' is an idiomatic expression meaning a period of intense conflict or struggle.
  • 此物争出投城隍及所在民家 - '此物' refers to the bamboo rats, '争出' means they come out in large numbers, '投' means to throw or enter, and '城隍' refers to the city walls or protective spirits of the city. '所在民家' means the homes of the people.
  • 或穿墉坏城 - '穿墉' means to dig through walls, and '坏城' means to damage the city.
  • 或自门阈而入 - '门阈' refers to the threshold of a door, and '而入' means to enter.
  • 犬食不尽则并入人家房内 - '犬食不尽' means the dogs cannot eat all of it, and '并入人家房内' means they also enter the houses.
  • 秦民之口腹饫焉 - '口腹' refers to the mouth and stomach, and '饫焉' means they are well-fed or satisfied.
  • 忽有童謡曰 - '童謡' means children's songs or rhymes, and '曰' means 'says' or 'says that.'
  • 引黑牛 - '' is repeated for emphasis, and '引黑牛' means leading a black bull.
  • 天差不自由 - '天差' means heavenly decree or fate, and '不自由' means not free or constrained.
  • 但看戊寅歳在蜀江头 - '但看' means to look at or observe, '戊寅歳' refers to the year of the Earth Tiger (a specific year in the Chinese zodiac), and '在蜀江头' means the bone is thrown into the head of the Shu River.
  • 智者不能议之 - '智者' means wise people, and '不能议之' means they cannot discuss or understand it.
  • 庚午歳大梁同州节度使刘知俊叛梁入秦家于天水 - '庚午歳' refers to the year of the Earth Horse, '大梁' is a city name, '同州' is another place name, '节度使' means military governor, '刘知俊' is a person's name, '叛梁' means rebel against the Later Liang, '入秦' means enter Qin, and '家于天水' means settle in Tianshui.
  • 天水破流入蜀居数年间 - '天水破' means Tianshui was captured, '流入蜀' means they moved to Shu, and '居数年间' means they stayed there for several years.
  • 蜀人又謡曰 - '蜀人' means people of Shu, and '又謡曰' means another rhyme or song was sung.
  • 黑牛无系绊 - '黑牛' refers to the black bull, '无系绊' means without restraint or tether.
  • 防绳一时断 - '防绳' means a restraining rope, and '一时断' means it breaks suddenly.
  • 伪蜀先主闻之惧曰 - '伪蜀先主' refers to the first ruler of the false Shu state, '闻之' means upon hearing it, and '惧曰' means he was afraid and said.
  • 黑牛者刘之小字 - '黑牛' is a nickname, and '刘之小字' means Liu's pet name.
  • 防绳者吾子孙之名也 - '防绳' is another nickname, and '吾子孙之名也' means it is the name of my descendants.
  • 葢前辈连宗字后辈连承字为名 - '葢' means because or since, '前辈' means ancestors, '连宗字' means to use the clan name, '后辈' means descendants, and '连承字' means to use the inherited name.
  • 防绳与宗承音同 - '防绳' and '宗承' sound the same.
  • 吾老矣得不为子孙之患乎 - '吾老矣' means I am old, '得不为' means will not be, and '子孙之患' means a problem for my descendants.
  • 于是害刘公以厌之 - '于是' means therefore, '害刘公' means to harm or kill Liu, and '以厌之' means to satisfy or appease it.
  • 明年歳在戊寅先主不豫合眼刘公在目前 - '明年' means the next year, '歳在戊寅' refers to the year of the Earth Tiger, '先主不豫' means the first ruler was ill, '合眼' means closed eyes, and '刘公在目前' means Liu appeared before his eyes.
  • 蜀人惧之遂粉刘之骨入于蜀江先主寻崩 - '蜀人惧之' means the people of Shu were afraid, '遂粉刘之骨' means they ground Liu's bones into powder, '入于蜀江' means they threw it into the Shu River, and '先主寻崩' means the first ruler soon died.
  • 议者方知者刘也 - '议者' means those who discussed, '方知' means finally realized, and '者刘也' means the bamboo rat was actually Liu.
  • 黑牛者刘之小字戊寅歳骨入于蜀江之应 - '黑牛者刘之小字' means the black bull was Liu's nickname, '戊寅歳骨入于蜀江' means in the year of the Earth Tiger, the bones were thrown into the Shu River, and '之应' means this was the fulfillment of the prophecy.

  • 恒以疾不送迎宾客 - Li Ying always used an excuse of illness to avoid sending people to welcome or see off guests.

  • 惟陈仲弓来辄乘轝出门迎之 - Only when Chen Zhonggong came would Li Ying personally go out to meet him in a carriage, showing special respect.
  • 膺每作一文出手门下共争之不得堕地 - Describes the eagerness of Li Ying's disciples to get his writings as soon as they were written, so much so that they would fight over them before they hit the ground.
  • 求仁得仁又谁恨也 - A phrase expressing contentment with one's fate, suggesting that if one seeks virtue and finds it, there is no reason to regret anything.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This long sentence describes the luxurious lifestyle of the Prince of Gaoyang, with his meals costing tens of thousands of coins and featuring delicacies from both sea and land.
  • This sentence is a quote from Li Chong, comparing the Prince of Gaoyang's meal to his own thousand-day meal.
  • This sentence describes Li Chong's wealth and frugality, with his meals consisting only of leek soup and pickled leeks.
  • This long sentence describes a story about a man from Nanyang who was very wealthy but extremely frugal, and how his son-in-law drank all his wine in one go, leading to a family dispute and murder.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '蛮陬乡国数千关河阻隔' emphasizes the geographical isolation, but the exact number of mountains and rivers is not specified.
  • The concessive phrase '况此官已满后任难期' introduces the main argument, emphasizing the uncertainty of the next appointment.
  • The clause '以保安之不才厄选曹之格限' explains the reasons for the uncertainty, combining personal incompetence with institutional rigidity.
  • The clause '更思微禄岂有望焉' expresses a sense of despair, questioning the possibility of any hope.
  • The clause '将归老丘园转死沟壑' presents two possible outcomes: a peaceful retirement or a tragic end.
  • The long sequence of clauses '侧闻吾子急人之忧不遗乡曲之情忽垂特达之眷' praises the recipient's concern for others and their special favor.
  • The long sequence of clauses '使保安得执鞭弭以奉周旋录及细微薄霑功効承兹凯入得预末班' outlines the benefits of the recipient's favor, from humble service to a small share of merit.
  • The clause '是吾子丘山之恩即保安铭镂之日非敢望也' emphasizes the depth of gratitude and the permanence of the record.
  • The clause '愿为图之唯照其欵诚而寛其造次专策驽蹇以望招擕' expresses a desire to consider the matter, asking for understanding and guidance.
  • The long sequence of clauses '仲翔得书深感之即言于李将军召为管记未至而蛮贼转逼李将军至姚州与战破之乘胜深入蛮覆而败之李身死军没仲翔为虏' describes the events leading to Zhong Xiang's capture and the military actions of General Li.
  • The clause '蛮夷利汉财物其没落者皆通音耗令其家赎之人三十匹' explains the barbarians' motivations and the ransom system.
  • The clause '保安既至姚州适值军没迟留未返而仲翔于蛮' describes the timing and circumstances of the events, leading to the final situation.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '蔡邕评陈蕃李膺先后曰陈仲举彊于犯上难李元礼严于挕下易仲举三君之下' is a long, continuous statement where the subject (Cai Yong) and the objects (Chen Fan and Li Ying) are introduced, followed by a series of comparisons and evaluations. The sentence structure is somewhat elliptical, with multiple clauses linked together.
  • The sentence '顾邵尝独谓庞士元曰闻子知人吾与足下孰愈' introduces a dialogue between Gu Shao and Pang Shiyuan, with a rhetorical question asking for a comparison between the two speakers.
  • The sentence '诸觊瑾兄弟各事一国时以蜀得其龙吴得其虎魏得其狗' is a continuous statement where the brothers Zhu Ji and Jin are described as each serving one country, with a metaphorical description of the states Shu, Wu, and Wei.

  • 良马须逸鞅泛驾 - Another metaphorical expression. '良马' means a good horse, '逸鞅' refers to a loose bridle, and '泛驾' means to run wild. This suggests that even a good horse needs to be controlled to be useful.

  • 沿边藩镇兵士数万 - Means 'several tens of thousands of soldiers from the border regions.'

  • 犬戎列阵于川 - Means 'the Dog Rong (a nomadic tribe) set up their array on the river.'
  • 生骑马数千匹 - Means 'several thousand live horses.'
  • 伏藏山谷 - Means 'hidden in the mountains and valleys.'
  • 既而得于谋者 - Means 'later, through strategic planning.'
